question_text: Let (3, 6)T, (4, 4)T, (5, 7)T and (4, 7)T be four independent observations from a

options.B: ivariate normal distribution with the mean vector µ and the covariance matrix Σ. Let ˆµ and ˆΣ be the maximum likelihood estimates of µ and Σ, respectively, based on these observations. Then ˆΣˆµ is equal to

solution:
The maximum likelihood estimates (MLE) for the mean vector µ and the covariance matrix
Σ of a bivariate normal distribution can be computed from the sample data. Let’s break down
the steps involved.
Step 1: Calculate the sample mean vector ˆµ.
The sample mean vector ˆµ is the average of the observed data points. We are given the
following four observations:
(3, 6)T, (4, 4)T, (5, 7)T, (4, 7)T
To compute the sample mean vector ˆµ, we take the average of the x-coordinates and the
y-coordinates separately.

Quick Tip
To calculate the maximum likelihood estimates of the mean and covariance, first find
the sample mean vector and sample covariance matrix. Then, use matrix multiplication
to obtain the required estimates.
